mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for equities, compressing valuations and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points over two months signals labor market deterioration, reducing corporate earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances, invalidating the labor deterioration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and wealth effects, driving positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and reduced economic activity, triggering short-term negative market sentiment.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 5-day returns in greater than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real rates turn negative, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative real rates correlate with positive 20-day returns in greater than 65% of instances, breaking the risk premium channel.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue projections, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% signals recessionary conditions, causing immediate repricing of equity valuations and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: GDP contractions trigger positive 20-day returns in greater than 50% of historical occurrences, negating the recession signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling employment indicates productivity shocks or automation, creating short-term market uncertainty and negative returns.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-PAYEMS divergence yields positive 5-day returns in greater than 60% of cases, invalidating the uncertainty channel.
